value.market_read: Headline: Crude -7.97 mb and gasoline +3.36 mb drew while distillate built 1.50 mb and jet built 0.44 mb. U.S. refinery utilization climbed to 94.7% (+0.20 pp w/w). Driver: PADD IV refinery utilization snapped +12.60 pp to 90.9%, the headline supply-side mover this week. Cushing fell to 22.4 mb (-0.58 mb w/w; -6.68 mb over 5 weeks) — operational tank-bottom is ~25 mb. Refiner crude inputs climbed 0.65 mb/d to 16.97 mb/d. Trade flows: Crude exports averaged 5.07 mb/d on 4-week basis (-0.08 mb/d vs prior 4-week window — multi-year high pace) while crude imports fell to 5.65 mb/d (-0.21 mb/d). Gasoline demand 4-wk avg 8.90 mb/d (-0.05 mb/d w/w) as driving season ramps. Watch next week: Memorial Day gasoline pull, Cushing trajectory vs 25 mb operational floor (22.4 mb currently — within -2.6 mb of the line), June 1 Atlantic hurricane season start with PADD III running near 98.1% capacity, and whether crude exports can sustain the 5.1 mb/d pace.

padd.padd1: Gasoline built 3.40 mb, refinery utilization eased -1.90 pp to 83.5%. Distillate also drew 1.46 mb. Watch NY Harbor RBOB structure and Colonial Pipeline nominations into Memorial Day.
padd.padd2: Distillate built 1.42 mb, refinery utilization climbed +1.20 pp to 97.1%. Crude also drew 0.84 mb. Cushing now at 22.4 mb (-0.58 mb w/w) — only -2.6 mb above the ~25 mb operational floor; watch WTI prompt structure.
padd.padd3: Crude drew 6.66 mb; refinery utilization steady at 98.1%. Distillate also built 1.29 mb. Hurricane risk window opens June 1 with refineries near peak; watch LOOP sour differentials and Asian crude buying pace.
padd.padd4: Crude drew 1.03 mb, refinery utilization climbed +12.60 pp to 90.9%. Bakken/DJ basin differentials should track PADD IV crude demand recovery.
padd.padd5: Crude built 1.02 mb, refinery utilization climbed +0.70 pp to 82.3%. CARB-spec isolation, ongoing Phillips 66 Rodeo conversion impact, and LA Basin margins remain the swing factors.
